title: श्री हनुमान स्तवन
devanagariText:
॥ श्री हनुमान स्तवन ॥

गोष्पदीकृतवारीशं मशकीकृतराक्षसम्।
रामायणमहामालारत्नं वन्दे‌ऽनिलात्मजम्॥१॥

अञ्जनानन्दनं वीरं जानकीशोकनाशनम्।
कपीशमक्षहन्तारं वन्दे लङ्काभयङ्करम्॥२॥

उल्लङ्घ्य सिन्धोः सलिलं सलीलं
यः शोकवह्निं जनकात्मजायाः।
आदाय तेनैव ददाह लङ्कां
नमामि तं प्राञ्जलिराञ्जनेयम्॥३॥

मनोजवं मारुततुल्यवेगं
जितेन्द्रियं बुद्धिमतां वरिष्ठम्।
वातात्मजं वानरयूथमुख्यं
श्रीरामदूतं शरणं प्रपद्ये॥४॥

आञ्जनेयं महावीरं ब्रह्मविष्णुशिवात्मकम्।
स्वयं रामकथां देवं वन्दे यत् काम पूरणम्॥५॥

अतुलितबलधामं हेमशैलाभदेहं
दनुजवनकृशानुं ज्ञानिनामग्रगण्यम्।
सकलगुणनिधानं वानराणामधीशं
रघुपतिप्रियभक्तं वातजातं नमामि॥६॥

यत्र यत्र रघुनाथकीर्तनं
तत्र तत्र कृतमस्तकाञ्जलिम्।
बाष्पवारिपरिपूर्णलोचनं
मारुतिं नमत राक्षसान्तकम्॥७॥

बुद्धिर्बलं यशो धैर्यं निर्भयत्वमरोगता।
अजाड्यं वाक्पटुत्वं च हनुमत्स्मरणाद् भवेत्॥८॥

॥ इति श्री हनुमान स्तवनं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Hanuman Stavan ||

Goshpadikritavaarisham Mashakikritaraakshasam.
Raamaayanamahaamaalaaratnam Vande'nilaatmajam.||1||

Anjanananandanam Veeram Jaanakeeshokanashanam.
Kapeeshamaksahantaaram Vande Lankaabhayankaram.||2||

Ullanghya Sindhoh Salilam Saleelam
Yah Shokavahnim Janakaatmajaayaah.
Aadaaya Tenaiva Dadaaha Lankaam
Namaami Tam Praanjaliraanjaneyam.||3||

Manojavam Maarutatulyavegam
Jitendriyam Buddhimataam Varishtham.
Vaataatmajam Vaanarayoothamukhyam
ShreeRaamadootam Sharanam Prapadye.||4||

Aanjaneyam Mahaaveeram Brahmavishnu Shivaatmakam.
Swayam Raamakathaam Devam Vande Yat Kaama Pooranam.||5||

Atulitabaladhaaram Hemashailaabhadheham
Danujavanakrishaanum Jnaaninamagragannyam.
Sakalagunanidhanam Vaanaraanaamadhisham
Raghupatipriabhaktam Vaatajaatam Namaami.||6||

Yatra Yatra Raghunaathakeertanam
Tatra Tatra Kritamastakaaanjalim.
Baashpavaariparipoornalochanam
Maarutim Namata Raakshasaantakam.||7||

Buddhirbalam Yasho Dhairyam Nirbhayatvamarogata.
Ajaadyam Vaakpatutvancha Hanumattsmaranaad Bhavet.||8||

|| Iti Shri Hanuman Stavanam Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
(1) जिन्होंने समुद्र को गोष्पद (गाय के खुर का चिह्न) के समान छोटा कर दिया, राक्षसों को मच्छर के समान तुच्छ किया, ऐसे रामायण की महामाला के रत्न पवनपुत्र को मैं वन्दन करता हूँ।

(2) अञ्जना के आनन्द, वीर, जानकी के शोक को मिटाने वाले, वानरों के स्वामी, अक्ष का वध करने वाले, लंका को भयभीत करने वाले को मैं वन्दन करता हूँ।

(3) जिन्होंने सहज ही समुद्र का जल लाँघकर, जनकपुत्री के शोकरूपी अग्नि को लेकर उसी से लंका को जला दिया, उन आञ्जनेय को मैं हाथ जोड़कर नमन करता हूँ।

(4) मन के समान वेगवान, पवन के तुल्य गतिशील, जितेन्द्रिय, बुद्धिमानों में श्रेष्ठ, वायुपुत्र, वानर-समूह के प्रमुख, श्रीराम के दूत की मैं शरण लेता हूँ।

(5) आञ्जनेय, महावीर, ब्रह्मा-विष्णु-शिव स्वरूप, स्वयं राम कथा के देवता और सभी कामनाओं को पूर्ण करने वाले को मैं वन्दन करता हूँ।

(6) अतुलित बल के धाम, सुवर्ण पर्वत के समान देह, दानवों के वन को जलाने वाली अग्नि, ज्ञानियों में अग्रगण्य, सकल गुणों के निधान, वानरों के अधीश, रघुपति के प्रिय भक्त पवनपुत्र को मैं नमन करता हूँ।

(7) जहाँ-जहाँ रघुनाथ का कीर्तन होता है, वहाँ-वहाँ जो हाथ जोड़कर मस्तक झुकाए और आँसू भरे नेत्रों से उपस्थित रहते हैं, उन राक्षसों का अन्त करने वाले मारुति को नमन करो।

(8) बुद्धि, बल, यश, धैर्य, निर्भयता, आरोग्य, अजड़ता और वाक्पटुता — ये सब हनुमत् स्मरण से प्राप्त होते हैं।
benefits: Invokes intelligence, strength, fame, and courage
Traditionally recited for removal of fear and bestows perfect health
Develops eloquence and sharpness of mind
Invokes Hanuman's protection as Ram Doot
Recitation fills the heart with devotion to Lord Rama
Traditionally recited for removal of negative energies and evil influences
description: Hanuman Stavan (श्री हनुमान स्तवन) is a sacred prayer dedicated to Lord Hanuman. This revered text originates from Traditional Sanskrit Hymn. Devotees recite this prayer to recitation fills the heart with devotion to Lord Rama and destroy negative energies and evil influences. It is also said to invoke Hanuman's protection as Ram Doot. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Hanuman Jayanti. With 8 verses and a reading time of about 6 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Hanuman Stavan in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Hanuman Stavan in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning and transliteration. Eight powerful verses praising Lord Hanuman's valor, wisdom, and devotion to Rama.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Hanuman Stavan and what is its significance?
seoFaqs.answer: The Hanuman Stavan (श्री हनुमान स्तवन) is a classical Sanskrit invocation of Lord Hanuman comprising eight powerful shlokas drawn from the most celebrated Hanuman dhyana verses preserved in Sanskrit devotional tradition. Unlike the Awadhi Hanuman Chalisa or Bajrang Baan of Tulsidas, the Stavan is composed in pure Sanskrit and contains the most universally chanted Hanuman shlokas across the Vaishnava, Smarta and Shaiva traditions: 'Goshpadi-krita-varisham', 'Manojavam Maruta-tulya-vegam', 'Atulita-bala-dhamam', and the famous phalashruti verse 'Buddhir-balam yasho dhairyam'. The Stavan serves as the standard dhyana sequence opening any Hanuman puja, parayana or sadhana — establishing the visual form of Hanuman in the meditator's mind through eight angles of glorification. (Sources: classical Hanuman paddhati; Vaishnava puja tradition; Anjaneya stuti tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Manojavam Maruta-tulya-vegam?
seoFaqs.answer: The verse 'Manojavam maruta-tulya-vegam, jitendriyam buddhimatam varishtham, vatatmajam vanara-yutha-mukhyam, Shri Rama-dutam sharanam prapadye' is among the most universally recited Hanuman shlokas — also appearing as the dhyana-shloka of the Sundara Kanda. Its meaning: 'I take refuge (sharanam prapadye) in Shri Rama's messenger (Rama-dutam) — who is swift as the mind (manojavam), equal in speed to the wind (maruta-tulya-vegam), the conqueror of the senses (jitendriyam), foremost among the wise (buddhimatam varishtham), the son of Vayu (vatatmajam), and the chief of the vanara hosts (vanara-yutha-mukhyam).' The shloka encapsulates Hanuman's six supreme qualities: speed of mind, speed of body, sense-control, supreme intellect, divine lineage, and leadership — making it the definitive sharanagati (surrender) verse for all Hanuman devotees. (Sources: Valmiki Ramayana Sundara Kanda; classical Hanuman dhyana paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the famous Atulita-bala-dhamam shloka in the Hanuman Stavan?
seoFaqs.answer: The verse 'Atulita-bala-dhamam hema-shailabha-deham, danuja-vana-krishanum jnaninam-agra-ganyam, sakala-guna-nidhanam vanaranam-adhisham, raghupati-priya-bhaktam vatajatam namami' is one of the supreme Hanuman dhyana shlokas in the Sanskrit tradition. Its meaning: 'I bow to the son of Vayu (vatajatam) — the abode of incomparable strength (atulita-bala-dhamam), whose body resembles the golden Meru mountain (hema-shailabha-deham), the fire to the forest of demons (danuja-vana-krishanum), foremost among the wise (jnaninam-agra-ganyam), the treasury of all virtues (sakala-guna-nidhanam), the lord of the vanaras (vanaranam-adhisham), and the beloved bhakta of Raghupati (raghupati-priya-bhaktam).' This verse is recited as the standard opening of the Hanuman Chalisa, Sundara Kanda parayana, and almost every Hanuman puja in the Smarta and Vaishnava traditions. (Sources: Ramcharitmanas; classical Sanskrit Hanuman dhyana; Sundara Kanda paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of the phalashruti 'Buddhir-balam yasho dhairyam'?
seoFaqs.answer: The eighth and final verse 'Buddhir-balam yasho dhairyam, nirbhayatvam-arogata, ajadyam vakpatutvam cha hanumat-smaranad bhavet' is the great phalashruti (statement of fruits) of Hanuman-smarana — and is among the most-quoted Sanskrit verses in all of Hanuman bhakti. Its meaning: 'Eight supreme fruits arise from the very remembrance of Hanuman: (1) Buddhi — sharp intellect; (2) Balam — physical and mental strength; (3) Yashas — fame and reputation; (4) Dhairyam — patience, courage and steadiness; (5) Nirbhayatvam — fearlessness; (6) Arogata — perfect health; (7) Ajadyam — alertness, freedom from inertia and dullness; (8) Vakpatutvam — eloquence and mastery of speech.' Tradition holds that mere smarana (remembrance) of Hanuman — even without elaborate puja — bestows these eight phalas. The verse is among the most powerful single shlokas to recite for personal development and overall well-being. (Sources: classical Hanuman dhyana paddhati; Sanskrit stotra-mala tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Goshpadikrita-varisham in the opening verse?
seoFaqs.answer: The opening verse 'Goshpadi-krita-varisham mashaki-krita-rakshasam, ramayana-maha-mala-ratnam vande'nila-atmajam' contains two of the most striking Sanskrit images for Hanuman's prowess: (1) 'Goshpadi-krita-varisham' — 'the one who reduced the ocean (varisha = waters/ocean) to a goshpada (the small puddle in a cow's hoof-print)' — meaning that for Hanuman, leaping the hundred-yojana ocean to Lanka was no greater feat than stepping over a tiny puddle; (2) 'Mashaki-krita-rakshasam' — 'the one who reduced the rakshasas to mashaka (mosquitoes)' — meaning that the entire demon army of Lanka was no more than swarming mosquitoes for him; (3) 'Ramayana-maha-mala-ratnam' — 'the central jewel (ratna) in the great garland (maha-mala) that is the Ramayana' — declaring Hanuman as the supreme jewel of the entire Ramayana epic. The verse closes 'Vande'nilatmajam' — 'I bow to the son of Anila (Vayu).' (Sources: classical Hanuman dhyana paddhati; Anjaneya stuti tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits are attributed to regular recitation of the Hanuman Stavan?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition attributes the following phala to sincere Hanuman Stavan recitation, with the eighth verse providing the canonical phalashruti: (1) Buddhi — sharpening of intellect, useful for students and decision-makers; (2) Balam — increase in physical strength and inner mental fortitude; (3) Yashas — growth of fame, reputation and credibility in the world; (4) Dhairya — cultivation of patience and steadiness in adversity; (5) Nirbhayatva — destruction of all fears, especially fear of death and unseen forces; (6) Arogata — bestowal of perfect health and freedom from disease; (7) Ajadya — removal of dullness, lethargy, and depressive heaviness of mind; (8) Vakpatutva — eloquence in speech, success in interviews, examinations, debates and public speaking. Additionally: protection as Ram-doot, deepening of Rama-bhakti, and removal of negative energies and evil influences. The Stavan is short enough for daily recitation yet potent enough to constitute a complete daily Hanuman sadhana. (Sources: Hanuman Stavan phalashruti verse 8; classical paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the best time to recite the Hanuman Stavan?
seoFaqs.answer: The most auspicious times for Hanuman Stavan recitation: (1) Brahma muhurta (96 minutes before sunrise) for daily Hanuman upasana — the Stavan's brevity makes it ideal for daily morning practice; (2) every Tuesday (Mangalvar) — the principal Hanuman day, with full puja, sindoor offering, and chola; (3) every Saturday (Shanivar) — Hanuman is the principal pacifier of Shani (Saturn), and Saturday recitation reduces Shani's malefic effects; (4) Hanuman Jayanti — Chaitra Purnima (the supreme Hanuman birthday, observed differently as Margashirsha Krishna Chaturdashi in some southern traditions); (5) before commencing Sundara Kanda parayana, Hanuman Chalisa path, or any Hanuman anushthana — the Stavan serves as the dhyana-shloka opening; (6) before any difficult task — interviews, exams, surgery, court appearances, long journeys; (7) at sunset during the daily aarti; (8) during Ekadashi if Hanuman upasana is part of one's daily practice. Daily recitation 7 or 11 times is widely recommended. (Sources: Drikpanchang Hanuman calendar; classical Hanuman paddhati.)
